course.title: Lesson 24: A Skeleton in the Cupboard

segments.text: A Skeleton in the Cupboard.

translations.zh-Hans: 橱柜里的骷髅。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Who was Sebastian?

translations.zh-Hans: 塞巴斯蒂安是谁？

segments.text: We often read in novels how a seemingly respectable person or family has some terrible secret which has been concealed from strangers for years.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们常在小说中读到，一个表面体面的人或家庭藏着某个可怕秘密，多年来一直不为外人所知。

segments.text: The English language possesses a vivid saying to describe this sort of situation.

translations.zh-Hans: 英语中有一句形象生动的说法，用来描述这种情形。

segments.text: The terrible secret is called a skeleton in the cupboard.

translations.zh-Hans: 这种可怕的秘密叫作“橱柜里的骷髅”。

segments.text: At some dramatic moment in the story, the terrible secret becomes known and a reputation is ruined.

translations.zh-Hans: 故事到了某个戏剧性时刻，秘密被揭开，名誉随之毁于一旦。

segments.text: The reader's hair stands on end when he reads in the final pages of the novel that the heroine, a dear old lady who had always been so kind to everybody, had, in her youth, poisoned every one of her five husbands.

translations.zh-Hans: 当读者在小说最后几页读到，那位女主人公——一个一向对所有人都很亲切的可爱老太太——年轻时竟毒死过五任丈夫时，不禁毛骨悚然。

segments.text: It is all very well for such things to occur in fiction.

translations.zh-Hans: 这样的事发生在小说里倒也无妨。

segments.text: To varying degrees, we all have secrets which we do not want even our closest friends to learn.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们每个人或多或少都有不愿让哪怕最亲密朋友知道的秘密。

segments.text: But few of us have skeletons in the cupboard.

translations.zh-Hans: 但我们很少有人真有“橱柜里的骷髅”。

segments.text: The only person I know who has a skeleton in the cupboard is George Carlton, and he is very proud of the fact.

translations.zh-Hans: 我认识的人中，只有乔治·卡尔顿的橱柜里有一具骷髅，而且他对此非常自豪。

segments.text: George studied medicine in his youth.

translations.zh-Hans: 乔治年轻时学过医。

segments.text: Instead of becoming a doctor, however, he became a successful writer of detective stories.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而，他没有成为医生，而是成了一名成功的侦探小说作家。

segments.text: I once spent an uncomfortable weekend which I shall never forget at his house.

translations.zh-Hans: 我曾在他家度过一个令人不安、终生难忘的周末。

segments.text: George showed me to the guest room which, he said, was rarely used.

translations.zh-Hans: 乔治把我带到客房，说那里很少使用。

segments.text: He told me to unpack my things and then come down to dinner.

translations.zh-Hans: 他让我把行李打开收拾好，然后下楼吃饭。

segments.text: After I had stacked my shirts and underclothes in two empty drawers, I decided to hang one of the two suits I had brought with me in the cupboard.

translations.zh-Hans: 我把衬衫和内衣放进两个空抽屉后，决定把带来的两套西装中的一套挂进橱柜。

segments.text: I opened the cupboard door and then stood in front of it, petrified.

translations.zh-Hans: 我打开橱柜门，随即站在门前，吓得呆若木鸡。

segments.text: A skeleton was dangling before my eyes.

translations.zh-Hans: 一具骷髅悬挂在我眼前。

segments.text: The sudden movement of the door made it sway slightly and it gave me the impression that it was about to leap out at me.

translations.zh-Hans: 开门的突然动作使它微微摇摆，让我觉得它就要扑向我。

segments.text: Dropping my suit, I dashed downstairs to tell George.

translations.zh-Hans: 我扔下西装，冲下楼去告诉乔治。

segments.text: George was worse than a terrible secret.

translations.zh-Hans: 乔治家的秘密可比普通的可怕秘密更吓人。

segments.text: This was a real skeleton.

translations.zh-Hans: 这是一具真正的骷髅。

segments.text: But George was unsympathetic.

translations.zh-Hans: 但乔治毫不同情。

segments.text: Oh, that, he said with a smile as if he were talking about an old friend.

translations.zh-Hans: “哦，是它呀，”他微笑着说，仿佛在谈一位老朋友。

segments.text: That's Sebastian.

translations.zh-Hans: “那是塞巴斯蒂安。

segments.text: You forget that I was a medical student once upon a time.

translations.zh-Hans: 你忘了我从前是医学院学生。”
